task_completion.description: Open or create the specified Jupyter notebook
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Jupyter Notebook interface (in a browser or IDE) is open with the file path or name indicating '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/hedonic_model.ipynb'.

task_completion.description: Load the San Francisco public dataset and handle missing values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells showing the loading of the 'buildings' table from '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5' and the use of a method like `.dropna()` to drop NaN rows.

task_completion.description: Set up the OLS regression model with at least three features

task_completion.description: Display the regression summary including R-squared and coefficients
task_completion.visual_evidence: An output cell displaying a standard regression summary table (e.g., from statsmodels) that includes R-squared values, coefficients, std errors, and p-values.

task_completion.description: Export the model coefficients to a CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells showing the extraction of variable names, coefficients, standard errors, and p-values, and saving them to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/hedonic_coefficients.csv', or a file explorer/terminal showing this file exists.

task_completion.description: Save a scatter plot of predicted vs actual values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells generating a scatter plot comparing predicted prices to actual 'residential_sales_price' and saving it to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/hedonic_scatter.png', or the image file itself visible in the output directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually executed the code in the Jupyter Notebook
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for execution count numbers (e.g., `[1]`, `[2]`) next to the code cells and corresponding outputs directly below them, rather than just typing code without running it.

integrity.description: Verify the regression summary is genuinely generated by the model
integrity.visual_evidence: The regression summary table should look like a standard library output (e.g., statsmodels) and not be manually printed or hardcoded text.

integrity.description: Verify the scatter plot is generated from the model's predictions
integrity.visual_evidence: The plotting code should reference the actual model prediction variables and the target variable, rather than plotting dummy data or downloading an external image.
